cycle_label: GOV-04 — governance-thread standalone sprint · pool telemetry validation + first batch ownerless-work execution · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-04 = 4). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-03 closed fully (health-refresh failure signal now working + NCAA baseball archived). GOV-04 opened from a fresh cycle-29 governance-backlog sweep (L01) and establishes pool telemetry: cycle.json now tracks pool-item state changes in real-time (carryover_resolved_in_cycle_29 section). L02 executes the first test batch (WS-B2 Nextcloud content-sync + XDG portal units), validating the pool-item triage + execution pattern.
goals: L01 — fresh cycle-29 governance-backlog sweep. DONE. Headline: cycle-09 workspace has two ancient deferred ownerless items (WS-B2 Nextcloud content-sync, XDG portal systemd units). Also found: cycle-29 in-scope governance items (3 sync-mechanism holes, instrument-gaps F2/F3/F4, DellaTech externalizables) queued behind initial pool-batch work. Pool telemetry established via cycle.json carryover_resolved_in_cycle_29 section.

L02 — first pool batch execution (WS-B2, XDG-portal). IN PROGRESS. WS-B2 triage: verify context, reason for deferral, decide strike/defer/complete + document. XDG-portal: verify orphaned status, remove from systemd --user, archive unit files, document as strike-ledger tombstone. Validate pool-item telemetry progression through L02 completion.

L03 — watches + cycle-29 carryover audit (pending). GOV-03's standing watches (dellatech-rag-indexer first-fire, health-refresh wrapper cron exercise) + cycle-29 carryover-item status glance before pooling next batch.
decisions_pending_operator: Pool-batch execution pace: WS-B2 + XDG-portal are the test batch. After L02 completes, operator decides: continue pooling cycle-29 governance items (sync-holes, instrument-gaps, externalizables) in rapid succession, or hold open and let the nephew trio absorb work in parallel? (Standing guidance: GOV takes ownerless backlog, never COLLECT-queue work.)

GOV-04 L02 completion criteria: both items triaged + executed (struck/deferred/completed) + telemetry progression logged + cycle-29 carryover_resolved section updated. No watches outstanding unless something fails.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: L02 is in-progress: triage WS-B2 (search cycle-09 context, identify deferral reason, decide outcome) and remove+archive XDG-portal units. Update cycle-29 cycle.json carryover_resolved section as items progress through triage → completion. After L02 done: glance at GOV-03 standing watches (dellatech-rag-indexer first-fire should have fired 05-15 02:32, health-refresh wrapper should have exercised in morning 07:15-07:35 cron batch + 08:10 checker). Then pool next batch of governance work or hold pending operator decision.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-04 opened after GOV-03 closed fully (health-refresh failure signal installed, NCAA baseball archived). Scoped from a fresh cycle-29 governance-backlog sweep per the operating model. Govenance-thread now registered in casey-junior PROJECT_ENDPOINTS for ops-dashboard visibility (deployment_id: 09c22323).
stakes: The governance thread is cross-BU and standalone — nesting its sprint-logs inside one BU's foreman (darntech) stranded them when the active cycle moved, and made /brief unrunnable from the governance workspace. Its own foreman-enabled home makes the thread continuously briefable and parallel-runnable alongside whatever product cycle is active. GOV-04 establishes pool-telemetry patterns (machine-readable work-item tracking in cycle.json) to make governance work-item progression visible for sweeps + dashboards.
notes: GOV-03 closed 2026-05-14 at a full-stop — health-refresh failure signal installed (wrapper + heartbeats + daily checker), NCAA baseball archived, all watches resolved GREEN. GOV-04 opened the same day from a fresh L01 governance-backlog sweep. Governance-thread now has its own Casey Junior deployment (09c22323) for ops dashboard integration. The governance-thread workspace is a checkout of dacumen.git — governance artifacts (charter amendments, ADRs) and governance operational tracking (.foreman + GOV sprint-logs) both belong to the dacumen repo.

carryover_decisions_at_open.gov03_dellatech_rag_indexer_watch: GOV-03 L01 found dellatech-rag-indexer.service never run (LAST = -). L04 verified: first fire (2026-05-15 02:32) succeeded cleanly, 1.9s, indexed 33 chunks. RESOLVED GREEN — no escalation needed.
carryover_decisions_at_open.gov03_health_refresh_wrapper_first_cron_: GOV-03 L03 rewrote 6 health-refresh crontab lines to use health-refresh-run.sh. L04 manually verified: all 6 pipelines reported 'last success 0h ago' (from L03 priming, still fresh). Checker exited 0 (all 26h-fresh). First real automatic exercise (tomorrow's 07:15-07:35 cron batch) will write new heartbeat timestamps. WATCHING but no escalation expected.
carryover_decisions_at_open.cycle_29_carryover_pool_telemetry: GOV-04 L01 established pool-telemetry tracking in darntech cycle-29 cycle.json: carryover_resolved_in_cycle_29 section now machine-reads work-item state. WS-B2 (Nextcloud content-sync) + XDG-portal units selected as L02 test batch. After L02 completion, operator decides pace for pooling remaining cycle-29 governance items (sync-holes, instrument-gaps, externalizables).
carryover_decisions_at_open.cascade_rc_rename_consumer_runtime_gap: Memory codified during GOV-02 L04: RC6 package rename not 'landed' until consumer venvs reconciled, not just source + image. Applies whenever a package rename touches deployed services + dev environments.
